# terrible_telekom_mms_icinga_director_icinga_scheduled_downtime (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `object_name` (String) Icinga object name for this downtime.

### Optional

- `append` (Boolean) Do not overwrite the whole object but instead append the defined properties. Note - Appending to existing vars, imports or any other list/dict is not possible. You have to overwrite the complete list/dict. Note - Variables that are set by default will also be applied, even if not set.
- `apply_to` (String) Whether this dependency should affect hosts or services Required if *state* is `present`.
- `assign_filter` (String) The filter where the downtime will take effect.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `author` (String) Name of the downtime author. Required if *state* is `present`.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `comment` (String) A descriptive comment for the downtime. Required if *state* is `present`.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disabled objects will not be deployed.
- `duration` (String) How long the downtime lasts. Only has an effect for flexible (non-fixed) downtimes. Time in seconds, supported suffixes include ms (milliseconds), s (seconds), m (minutes), h (hours) and d (days). To express "90 minutes" you might want to write 1h 30m
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fixed` (Boolean) Whether this downtime is fixed or flexible. If unsure please check the related documentation https://icinga.com/docs/icinga2/latest/doc/08-advanced-topics/#downtimes Required if *state* is `present`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `ranges` (String) The period which should be downtimed
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Apply feature state.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `with_services` (Boolean) Whether you only downtime the hosts or add some services with it.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
